title: Biomonitoring Content Summary for the Canadian Health Measures Survey - Canada.ca
content:
## Biomonitoring Content Summary for the Canadian Health Measures Survey
These chemicals have been measured or  are planned for measurement in biological samples, such as blood, urine, hair, or  pooled serum, collected as part of the Canadian Health Measures Survey (CHMS).  The chemicals were selected based on known or suspected health effects  resulting from exposure, level of public concern, evidence of exposure in the  Canadian population, and technical feasibility and cost of measurement. In  addition, factors such as emerging Health Canada priorities are considered  while selecting chemicals for inclusion in the CHMS. Summary data for these  chemicals have been released, or are planned for release, in the Canadian biomonitoring dashboard , as well as in biomonitoring fact sheets  and scientific articles. The table below indicates the age range of survey  participants for chemicals measured in biological samples. In addition to  biological samples, the table also indicates “Household” for chemicals measured  in household environmental samples, such as indoor air or tap water, collected  in a given CHMS cycle. Use the “Filter items” field to search the table and  narrow the information displayed.
